name: LoanMax Title Loans
description_short: LoanMax Title Loans at 2882 North Ave, Grand Junction, CO 81501 is Mon-Fri 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.9 stars across 304 reviews. Call +1 970-438-4410.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has operated for over 25 years as a vehicle title loan provider across multiple states. The company positions itself as helping "hardworking Americans use the equity in their vehicle to access the cash they need." The Southgate, Michigan location is one of many LoanMax branches, with nearby locations in Taylor, Garden City, Detroit, and Center Line within the Detroit metro area.

LoanMax's core service is auto title loans, where customers leverage their vehicle's title to access emergency cash up to $10,000. The process requires only a vehicle, photo ID, and clear vehicle title. The company accepts multiple payment methods including cash, check, debit card, and Western Union. The Southgate location operates Monday-Friday 10:00 AM–6:00 PM and Saturday 9:00 AM–2:00 PM. Customers can call the store directly at 734-237-7887 or the national line at 877-511-CASH.

LoanMax distinguishes itself through its long operational history and multi-state presence with standardized licensing in regulated states (Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, Nevada, Michigan). The website includes state-specific compliance information and consumer protection pamphlets, suggesting a regulated approach to title lending. The company also offers online account management (login/register functions) and online payment options.

Title loans are high-cost emergency credit products typically carrying substantial interest rates and fees. Borrowers risk vehicle repossession if they default. While LoanMax's licensing and state compliance disclosures are positive, title loans should be understood as expensive short-term solutions, not long-term financial products. Nevada's own consumer notice on the website explicitly warns that title loans are for short-term needs only.
pros: Quick cash access up to $10,000 with minimal documentation (vehicle, ID, title only)
Multiple payment methods accepted (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
Licensed and regulated in multiple states with published compliance information
Online account access and online payment capability available
Extended hours including Saturday service (9 AM–2 PM) for in-person visits
Multiple nearby locations in Detroit metro area (5 locations within 22 miles)
Clear disclosure of state-specific consumer protections and rate schedules
cons: Title loans carry high interest rates and fees; exact APR not disclosed on website
Risk of vehicle repossession if loan is not repaid
Loan approval contingent on vehicle evaluation and ability to repay (not guaranteed)
No information provided about alternative loan products or financial counseling
Website lacks transparency on typical loan terms, repayment schedules, or cost examples
best_for: Individuals with immediate cash needs who own a vehicle outright or with minimal lien
Borrowers who need emergency funds but cannot qualify for traditional unsecured loans
People who prefer in-person lending transactions in the Detroit metro area
diagnosis: LoanMax Title Loans is best for consumers facing genuine emergency cash needs who own a vehicle and have exhausted other borrowing options. However, borrowers must understand that title loans are expensive short-term credit products with substantial costs and significant default risk, including potential vehicle loss. This product is appropriate only for short-term liquidity gaps, not ongoing financial needs.
services: Same-day or next-day cash access (implied by emergency-cash category)
In-person lending at Southgate location (12850 Eureka Road)
Multiple payment method acceptance (cash, check, debit card, Western Union)
